problem.question: What exact theoretical question, definition, or conjecture are we trying to sharpen and validate with Lean-backed checks?
successDefinition: Produce a sharp formulation of the core problem
Arrive at a plausible theorem shape, conjecture, or obstruction
Develop a proof strategy that can survive Lean-backed validation where appropriate
Respect the user's background, resources, and explicit requirements
nonGoals: Do not auto-start implementation in src/ before user review
Do not simulate progress with vague definitions or cosmetic novelty
Do not keep weak conjectures alive after decisive negative evidence
Do not continue on paper-only proof claims when Lean-backed checks reveal blockers

rules: Do not start autonomous research until the user has provided research background and requirements
Every exploration pass must save notes, Lean-backed checks, attempted arguments, and interpretation
idea.md must be revised whenever the best current formulation changes
The loop must stop once the idea is professionalized and ready for user review

automation.notes: Theoretical projects stop at the user review gate. final_review may reopen earlier autonomous stages before user_review.

principles: One item should test one mechanism
Negative evidence should kill weak ideas
hardRules: If the central definitions cannot be stated crisply, simplify them
If the conjecture is too vague to attack or falsify, sharpen it before proceeding
If a counterexample or obstruction kills the claim, do not rescue it with ad hoc complexity
If a claim cannot survive the strongest available Lean-backed validation, treat that as real evidence
If the contribution would not survive honest expert scrutiny, sharpen it or kill it

userStories.title: Lock the exact theoretical question and win condition
userStories.hypothesis: A sharper problem framing prevents the loop from wandering into vague or unattackable claims.
constraints: The question must be explicit and falsifiable where possible
Success conditions must be honest about what counts as progress
acceptanceCriteria: Write the exact question, non-goals, and win condition
Record the research box and hard constraints clearly
Remove ambiguity about what the contribution would be

userStories.title: Clarify the key definitions and conceptual bottlenecks
userStories.hypothesis: A crisp conceptual framing exposes the real mechanism gap before conjecture shaping begins.
constraints: Do not overproduce definitions that are not needed
Tie each concept to a concrete role in the argument
acceptanceCriteria: State the key objects and definitions cleanly
Identify the main conceptual bottlenecks
Record what ambiguity still blocks theorem shaping

userStories.title: Study the most relevant prior work deeply
userStories.hypothesis: A targeted literature review will separate real conceptual gaps from fake novelty.
constraints: Focus on the strongest relevant work only
Explain what each result or method leaves unresolved
acceptanceCriteria: Document the strongest relevant prior work
Summarize each mechanism or argument shape
Identify at least one concrete gap worth pursuing

userStories.title: Draft the strongest plausible theorem or conjecture shape
userStories.hypothesis: A good target statement can usually be sharpened before heavy proof search if the problem framing is honest.
constraints: State the claim as sharply as current evidence allows
Do not hide uncertainty behind vague wording
acceptanceCriteria: Write the core claim crisply
Explain why the claim might matter
State the main evidence or intuition supporting it

userStories.title: Design the main proof strategy
userStories.hypothesis: A convincing proof strategy can be judged before exhaustive derivations if the mechanism is real.
constraints: Prefer the simplest credible proof route
Identify the main lemmas, reductions, or obstructions explicitly
acceptanceCriteria: Write the candidate proof strategy
List the key lemmas or subgoals
Record what would most likely break the argument

userStories.title: Run Lean-backed theorem and proof validation checks
userStories.hypothesis: Small, decisive Lean-backed checks can kill or sharpen a theoretical idea before it hardens into self-deception.
constraints: Use the configured lean4_skills_plus_lsp stack unless a hard blocker is found
Record attempted examples, obstructions, failed proof routes, and Lean-backed findings
acceptanceCriteria: Perform the planned Lean-backed checks
Save the attempted arguments, Lean-backed findings, and outcomes
Update idea.md as evidence sharpens or weakens the claim

userStories.title: Converge the final formulation
userStories.hypothesis: Once the main unknowns are resolved, the best move is to professionalize the formulation and stop.
constraints: Stop once the core conjecture is either credible or dead
Prepare a reviewer-ready formulation without making the final handoff decision yet
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite idea.md into a clean final version
Separate supported claims from remaining risks honestly
Leave final_review with a clean input package to judge

userStories.title: Run the final AI review and decide handoff versus rework
userStories.hypothesis: A final adversarial review should decide whether the current formulation deserves human review or another autonomous theory cycle.
constraints: Write the structured review panel before finalizing the memo
If autonomous rework is chosen, specify reopenStage and concrete reworkGoals
acceptanceCriteria: Fill the review panel with a structured conclusion
Write a final review memo consistent with the panel
Choose handoff_to_user or autonomous_rework honestly

userStories.title: Stop at the user review gate
userStories.hypothesis: A human review gate after final_review prevents the loop from drifting beyond justified belief.
constraints: Set automation.state to awaiting_user_review only after final_review says handoff_to_user
Do not auto-advance beyond this point
acceptanceCriteria: Leave the repository ready for human review
Record the stop condition in the control file
Stop the autonomous loop cleanly
